target.name: Harbinger
target.founder: John Harris, Phillip Weicker, Will Eberts

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

data.narrative:
Harbinger, a Series C transportation company founded by John Harris, Phillip Weicker, and Will Eberts, currently carries a post-money valuation of $500M against $358M in total capital raised. The relevant peer universe comprises 7 private frontier-tech transportation firms, including Einride, Rivian, The Boring Company, REGENT Craft, and ZeroAvia. No public comparables exist in this niche.

The stage-progression method is applicable here, using Harbinger's $500M last post-money valuation as the base and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C to next-stage advancement. This produces an implied valuation of $850M. The peer-median approach, however, does not yield sufficient analytical weight: while 4 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies yields fewer than three datapoints, making sector-wide median comparison unreliable and potentially misleading.

Given the methodological constraints, a single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $722M to $977M, with a median estimate of $850M. The 35% spread reflects uncertainty inherent in stage-progression analysis alone. Key risk factors include: the small and nascent peer set in frontier transportation, which limits validation of stage-step assumptions; execution risk in a capital-intensive sector; and the absence of public market anchors for discount-rate calibration. Additional data on Harbinger's unit economics, customer concentration, and burn trajectory would strengthen conviction in any final valuation.
